# UC-22.51.10 · OT Malware Protection Coverage and Detection Evidence

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check that every plant computer has up-to-date antivirus running and protecting in real time, and that someone scanned the computer in the last month. If anything is missing — or if the antivirus actually catches something — we ring the bell.*

---

## Description

Continuously evaluates malware-protection deployment, signature freshness, scan cadence, and real-time-protection status on every OT-capable endpoint in the production inventory, and aggregates detection volume from the last 24 hours.

## Value

OTCC inspectors require both 'every system has AV' and 'AV is actually working' — the trap is that an engineering workstation can have AV installed but with real-time protection disabled (a common workaround for vendor-tool interference), making the install evidence misleading. This UC checks all four aspects continuously and surfaces detections that need investigation.

## Implementation

(1) Onboard endpoint-protection telemetry from each AV product in use across the OT estate. (2) Maintain `oem_approved_av.csv` of which AV products each OEM has approved for use on their controllers/HMIs (e.g. Rockwell-approved list: Trend Micro OfficeScan, McAfee Endpoint Security). (3) Maintain `ot_av_required_flag` in the asset inventory — some passive sensors and embedded devices do not support AV and are not in scope. (4) Schedule UC-22.51.10 hourly. Route detections (any `detection_count_24h>0`) to ES notable.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.51.10` archived hourly to `audit_evidence` with `otcc_clause="OTCC-2-3-3-1"`. Each row carries (asset_key, asset_class, av_product, av_engine_version, av_signature_version, signature_age_hours, scan_last_completed_at, scan_age_hours, real_time_protection_status, detection_count_24h, quarantined_count_24h, coverage_gap_flag, signature_stale_flag, scan_overdue_flag, rtp_disabled_flag). Dashboard `NCA OTCC – Malware Protection` shows coverage map, signature freshness, scan cadence, and detection volume.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Disable real-time protection on a test engineering workstation in the lab. Within one hour UC-22.51.10 MUST flag the asset with `rtp_disabled_flag=true`, route an alert to the OT cybersecurity programme manager, and surface the asset on the dashboard.

### Negative scenario

Confirm a healthy engineering workstation with current signatures (<24h), real-time protection enabled, and a scan completed yesterday returns no flags.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- Splunk Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud.
- Endpoint protection ingestion from the canonical OT-approved products:
  - Microsoft Defender for Endpoint via Splunk Add-on Splunkbase 6207 (preferred for new estates).
  - CrowdStrike FDR via Splunk Add-on Splunkbase 5408 (preferred for hybrid IT/OT estates).
  - Trend Micro OfficeScan / Apex One via Splunkbase 4631 (common in plant-floor deployments).
  - McAfee ePO via Splunkbase 2310 (legacy estates).
  - Symantec Endpoint Protection via Splunkbase 2772 (legacy estates).
  - Vendor-specific OT EDR: Claroty CTD, Nozomi Guardian, Tenable.ot (passive AV status reporting), Industrial Defender ASM.
- `index=endpoint` (24-month retention), `index=audit_evidence`.
- Lookups:
  - `ot_asset_inventory_lookup` (KV Store, from UC-22.51.3) — add field `ot_av_required_flag` populated for every prod asset.
  - `oem_approved_av.csv` — owned by OT cybersecurity engineering. Columns: `asset_class`, `oem`, `approved_av_products` (mvfield).
  - `paused_av_during_maintenance.csv` — owned by OT operations.
  - `airgapped_av_attestation.csv` — owned by OT cybersecurity programme manager.
  - KV Store `otcc_22_51_10_exceptions` and `otcc_22_51_10_detections_whitelist`.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Microsoft Defender for Endpoint via the AAD-app modular input:

```ini
[microsoft_defender_endpoint://operational]
client_id = <aad-app-id>
tenant = <tenant>
client_secret = <key-vault-reference>
endpoint = https://api.security.microsoft.com
resources = machines, alerts
index = endpoint
sourcetype = MS-Defender:operational
interval = 600
```

Validate the four required fields:

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="MS-Defender:operational" earliest=-1d
| fieldsummary
| search field IN (asset_key, av_product, av_signature_version, signature_published_at, real_time_protection_status, scan_last_completed_at)
```

If `signature_published_at` is absent on Defender events, the API version is too old; upgrade the TA.

CrowdStrike FDR via Streaming API:

```ini
[crowdstrike_fdr://av_status]
client_id = <falcon-api-id>
client_secret = <key-vault-reference>
resource = host_visibility
index = endpoint
sourcetype = crowdstrike:av_status
```

For legacy McAfee ePO, DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) polls the EPOLeafNode and EPOClientAlerts tables every 30 minutes.

Volume guidance: a 1,000-host OT estate generates ~10–20 MB/day in AV status events, ~100 MB/day in detections.

## Step 2 — Create the search and alert

The search has five computational steps:

1. **Asset filter**: include only assets flagged `ot_av_required_flag=true` in `prod` environment.
2. **Latest state per asset**: collapse to the latest event per asset to avoid double-counting.
3. **Detection join**: outer-join the last 24 hours of `detected` / `quarantine` events to surface activity.
4. **Flag computation**:
   - `coverage_gap_flag` if AV product absent or not in OEM-approved list.
   - `signature_stale_flag` if signature >168h (7 days) — NCA OTCC requires 'kept current'; 7 days is a tighter local interpretation than the open standard. Sites that want stricter can lower to 72h.
   - `scan_overdue_flag` if last full scan >720h (30 days).
   - `rtp_disabled_flag` if real-time protection is not enabled.
5. **Result filter**: keep only flagged rows or rows with detections.

**Schedule**:

```ini
[UC-22.51.10: OT Malware Protection Coverage and Detection Evidence]
cron_schedule = 5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -2h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable, email
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.51.10,reg=NCA_OTCC,clause=OTCC-2-3-3-1
action.notable.param.rule_title = NCA OTCC malware-protection evidence gap or detection
action.notable.param.security_domain = endpoint
action.notable.param.severity = high
action.email.to = ot-cyber-programme@example.com
alert.severity = 4
```

Notable creation for `detection_count_24h>0` or `rtp_disabled_flag=true`. Other flags are persistent governance issues, routed to email + audit_evidence.

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Coverage check**: every asset with `ot_av_required_flag=true` should appear in the AV status feed in the last 24h:

```spl
(index=endpoint earliest=-1d) OR (| inputlookup ot_asset_inventory_lookup | where ot_av_required_flag="true")
| eval src=if(isnotnull(asset_class), "inventory", "av")
| stats values(src) AS sources BY asset_key
| where NOT mvfind(sources, "av")>=0
| stats count
```

Non-zero means AV is missing or its feed is broken; investigate.

**(b) Approved-product alignment**: every asset's AV must be in its OEM-approved list:

```spl
index=endpoint earliest=-1d
| lookup oem_approved_av.csv asset_class
| where NOT match(approved_av_products, av_product)
| stats count BY asset_class av_product
```

Non-zero means an asset has an unapproved AV — either the OEM's approved list has changed (update the lookup) or the wrong AV was installed.

**(c) Signature distribution**:

```spl
index=endpoint earliest=-1d
| stats count BY av_product av_signature_version
```

Look for stale signature versions concentrating in particular asset classes; that points to an AV update path blocked.

**(d) Positive control**: per `controlTest.positiveScenario`. Confirm `rtp_disabled_flag=true` fires within one hour.

**(e) Negative control**: per `controlTest.negativeScenario`.

## Step 4 — Operationalize

**Dashboard**: `NCA OTCC – Malware Protection`.

- **Row 1, single values**: 'RTP disabled now', 'Signatures >7 days', 'Scans >30 days', 'Detections in 24h'.
- **Row 2, choropleth**: coverage-gap count by plant/site, drilling into the asset list.
- **Row 3, timechart**: detection volume by `av_product` over 30 days.
- **Row 4, sortable table**: flagged assets with drill-through to vendor AV console and to the asset inventory record.
- **Row 5, signature-age histogram**: 0–168h buckets.

**Runbook for `detection_count_24h>0`** (owner: OT cybersecurity SOC, with OT engineering on call):

1. **Within 30 minutes**: pull the detection event detail (file hash, file path, parent process, user).
2. **Containment decision**: if asset is critical-criticality (`asset_criticality=critical`) and AV did not quarantine, do NOT auto-isolate — OT isolation can disrupt safety operations. Instead, contact OT operations to schedule manual containment.
3. **For non-critical assets**: if AV quarantined and the detection is a known commodity malware, proceed with isolation and remediation.
4. **Forensic preservation**: capture the file and surrounding process tree for analysis. Open a case in the OT SIRT system.
5. **Lessons learned**: feed back into UC-22.51.16 (incident detection) and UC-22.51.17 (incident reporting).

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting

**Real-time protection shows `disabled` on a particular vendor's HMI** — the vendor's HMI runtime is incompatible with RTP scanning, causing it to be deliberately disabled. Either:
- Migrate to an OEM-approved AV that the vendor has explicitly certified with RTP on (e.g. some Trend Micro builds), or
- Compensate with application whitelisting and document in `airgapped_av_attestation.csv` with `av_substitute="app_whitelisting"`.

**Signatures stale on a particular subnet** — the AV update path is blocked. Trace from the AV management server to the affected hosts; common causes: firewall rule changes, OT-DMZ proxy outage, expired certificate on the AV update relay.

**Detections never appear despite known-bad files seeded in lab** — the AV product is configured for 'monitor' mode rather than 'enforce' mode, common in early OT deployments. Move to 'enforce' for everything in `asset_criticality IN ("high","critical")` after coordination with OT operations.

**OT-specific failure modes**:

- **Vendor exclusion lists**: AV vendors and OEMs publish exclusion lists for OT software (e.g. Rockwell publishes Trend Micro exclusion rules for FactoryTalk). Missing the exclusion can cause AV-induced process disruption; verify the deployed AV exclusion list matches the current OEM publication. Track in `oem_av_exclusion_versions.csv`.
- **Engineering laptop transient state**: an engineer's laptop connecting to the OT-DMZ briefly may not report AV status until it has been on the network for 15 minutes. Filter out events where `host_seen_minutes<15` to suppress this transient.
- **Vendor remote-support tools tripping AV**: TeamViewer-style or vendor-proprietary remote-support tools often trip generic detections. Add the corresponding hash to the per-detection whitelist KV Store with documented vendor authorisation.
- **Sandbox / honeypot detections**: some OT cybersecurity programmes deploy honeypots on the OT network. AV detections on the honeypot are intentional; suppress via `asset_key IN (honeypot_assets.csv)`.

## SPL

```spl
(index=endpoint sourcetype IN ("MS-Defender:operational","crowdstrike:av_status","claroty:av:status","nozomi:guardian:av","trendmicro:ot_av","mcafee:epo:status","symantec:sep:status","tenable:ot:av") earliest=-2h@h)
| eval asset_key=coalesce(asset_key, host, computer_name)
| lookup ot_asset_inventory_lookup asset_key OUTPUT asset_class asset_criticality environment ot_av_required_flag
| where environment="prod" AND ot_av_required_flag="true"
| lookup oem_approved_av.csv asset_class OUTPUT approved_av_products
| stats latest(av_product) AS av_product latest(av_engine_version) AS av_engine_version latest(av_signature_version) AS av_signature_version latest(scan_last_completed_at) AS scan_last_completed_at latest(real_time_protection_status) AS real_time_protection_status latest(signature_published_at) AS signature_published_at BY asset_key asset_class
| eval signature_age_hours = round((now() - strptime(signature_published_at, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"))/3600, 1)
| eval scan_age_hours = round((now() - strptime(scan_last_completed_at, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"))/3600, 1)
| join asset_key type=outer [search (index=endpoint sourcetype IN ("MS-Defender:operational","crowdstrike:fdr","trendmicro:ot_av")) action="detected" OR action="quarantine" earliest=-24h | stats count(eval(action=="detected")) AS detection_count_24h count(eval(action=="quarantine")) AS quarantined_count_24h BY asset_key]
| eval coverage_gap_flag = if(isnull(av_product) OR av_product="" OR NOT match(approved_av_products, av_product), "true", "false")
| eval signature_stale_flag = if(signature_age_hours > 168, "true", "false")
| eval scan_overdue_flag = if(scan_age_hours > 720, "true", "false")
| eval rtp_disabled_flag = if(real_time_protection_status!="enabled", "true", "false")
| where coverage_gap_flag="true" OR signature_stale_flag="true" OR scan_overdue_flag="true" OR rtp_disabled_flag="true" OR detection_count_24h>0
| table asset_key asset_class av_product av_engine_version av_signature_version signature_age_hours scan_last_completed_at scan_age_hours real_time_protection_status detection_count_24h quarantined_count_24h coverage_gap_flag signature_stale_flag scan_overdue_flag rtp_disabled_flag
| sort - rtp_disabled_flag coverage_gap_flag scan_overdue_flag signature_stale_flag
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Coverage map: choropleth or heatmap by site/plant of `coverage_gap_flag=true` count. (Panel 2) Single values: 'RTP disabled now', 'Signatures >7 days', 'Scans >30 days'. (Panel 3) Detection volume timechart by `av_product` for the last 30 days. (Panel 4) Sortable table of every flagged asset.

## Known False Positives

1. **Embedded HMIs without AV surface** — some OEM HMI panels run a locked-down Windows IoT or Linux-based variant that does not support standard endpoint AV. Suppression: in the asset inventory, set `ot_av_required_flag=false` for these assets and document the compensating control (network containment, application whitelisting on the upstream gateway).

2. **Vendor maintenance windows that pause AV scans** — some vendors require AV scanning paused during the engineering work window (so as not to interfere with project download). Suppression: `paused_av_during_maintenance.csv` lists scheduled maintenance windows; the `scan_overdue_flag` is suppressed for assets whose CMDB entry overlaps an active window.

3. **Application-whitelisting in lieu of AV** — some OEMs prescribe application whitelisting (e.g. McAfee Application Control, Microsoft AppLocker) instead of signature AV. Suppression: list those assets in `airgapped_av_attestation.csv` with `av_substitute="app_whitelisting"` and the date of the most recent whitelisting attestation.

4. **Detection-during-validation false positive** — some industrial scripts and ladder-logic-extraction utilities can trip generic heuristic detections. Suppression: KV Store `otcc_22_51_10_detections_whitelist` keyed on `(file_hash, asset_class)` with `expires_at` 90-day rolling.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `otcc_22_51_10_exceptions` keyed on `asset_key` for asset-level exceptions and the per-detection KV store noted above.

## References

- [NCA OTCC — control list portal](https://nca.gov.sa/en/regulatory-documents/controls-list/3)
- [Rockwell Automation — AV/anti-malware policy guidance for control system PCs](https://www.rockwellautomation.com/en-us/trust-center/security-advisories.html)
- [Splunk CIM: Malware data model](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Malware)
